title: Apteranthes joannis
taxonomy: Apteranthes joannis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. It belongs to the genus Apteranthes, which is a small group of succulent plants. Apteranthes joannis is native to the island of Corsica, where it can be found growing in rocky areas. This plant is characterized by its unique, pale yellow flowers and thick, fleshy leaves. As a member of the Aizoaceae family, Apteranthes joannis is closely related to other succulent plants, such as Lithops and Conophytum.
